speaker: OTHELLO
text: You have seen nothing then?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Nor ever heard, nor ever did suspect.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Yes, you have seen Cassio and she together.

speaker: EMILIA
text: But then I saw no harm, and then I heard

speaker: EMILIA
text: Each syllable that breath made up between them.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What, did they never whisper?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Never, my lord.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Nor send you out o’ th’ way?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Never.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To fetch her fan, her gloves, her mask, nor nothing?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Never, my lord.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That’s strange.

speaker: EMILIA
text: I durst, my lord, to wager she is honest,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Lay down my soul at stake. If you think other,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Remove your thought. It doth abuse your bosom.

speaker: EMILIA
text: If any wretch have put this in your head,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Let heaven requite it with the serpent’s curse,

speaker: EMILIA
text: For if she be not honest, chaste, and true,

speaker: EMILIA
text: There’s no man happy. The purest of their wives

speaker: EMILIA
text: Is foul as slander.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Bid her come hither. Go.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: She says enough. Yet she’s a simple bawd

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That cannot say as much. This is a subtle whore,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: A closet lock and key of villainous secrets.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And yet she’ll kneel and pray. I have seen her do ’t.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: My lord, what is your will?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Pray you, chuck, come hither.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: What is your pleasure?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Let me see your eyes. Look in my face.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: What horrible fancy’s this?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Some of your function, mistress.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Leave procreants alone, and shut the door.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Cough, or cry “hem,” if anybody come.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Your mystery, your mystery! Nay, dispatch.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Upon my knees, what doth your speech import?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I understand a fury in your words,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: But not the words.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Why? What art thou?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Your wife, my lord, your true and loyal wife.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Come, swear it. Damn thyself,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Lest, being like one of heaven, the devils themselves

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Should fear to seize thee. Therefore be double damned.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Swear thou art honest.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Heaven doth truly know it.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Heaven truly knows that thou art false as hell.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: To whom, my lord? With whom? How am I false?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ah, Desdemon, away, away, away!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Alas the heavy day, why do you weep?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Am I the motive of these tears, my lord?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: If haply you my father do suspect

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: An instrument of this your calling back,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Lay not your blame on me. If you have lost him,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I have lost him too.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Had it pleased heaven

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To try me with affliction, had they rained

speaker: OTHELLO
text: All kind of sores and shames on my bare head,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Steeped me in poverty to the very lips,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Given to captivity me and my utmost hopes,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I should have found in some place of my soul

speaker: OTHELLO
text: A drop of patience. But alas, to make me

speaker: OTHELLO
text: A fixèd figure for the time of scorn

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To point his slow unmoving finger at—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Yet could I bear that too, well, very well.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But there where I have garnered up my heart,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Where either I must live or bear no life,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The fountain from the which my current runs

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Or else dries up—to be discarded thence,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Or keep it as a cistern for foul toads

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To knot and gender in—turn thy complexion there,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Patience, thou young and rose-lipped cherubin,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ay, there look grim as hell.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I hope my noble lord esteems me honest.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, ay, as summer flies are in the shambles,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That quicken even with blowing! O thou weed,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Who art so lovely fair, and smell’st so sweet

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That the sense aches at thee, would thou hadst ne’er been born!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Alas, what ignorant sin have I committed?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Was this fair paper, this most goodly book,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Made to write “whore” upon? What committed?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Committed? O thou public commoner,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I should make very forges of my cheeks

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That would to cinders burn up modesty,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Did I but speak thy deeds. What committed?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Heaven stops the nose at it, and the moon winks;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The bawdy wind that kisses all it meets

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Is hushed within the hollow mine of earth

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And will not hear ’t. What committed?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Impudent strumpet!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: By heaven, you do me wrong!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Are not you a strumpet?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: No, as I am a Christian!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: If to preserve this vessel for my lord

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: From any other foul unlawful touch

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Be not to be a strumpet, I am none.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What, not a whore?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: No, as I shall be saved.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Is ’t possible?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: O, heaven forgive us!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I cry you mercy, then.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I took you for that cunning whore of Venice

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That married with Othello.—You, mistress,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That have the office opposite to Saint Peter

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And keeps the gate of hell—you, you, ay, you!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: We have done our course. There’s money for your pains.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I pray you turn the key and keep our counsel.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Alas, what does this gentleman conceive?

speaker: EMILIA
text: How do you, madam? How do you, my good lady?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Faith, half asleep.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Good madam, what’s the matter with my lord?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: With who?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Why, with my lord, madam.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Who is thy lord?

speaker: EMILIA
text: He that is yours, sweet lady.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I have none. Do not talk to me, Emilia.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I cannot weep, nor answers have I none

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: But what should go by water. Prithee, tonight

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Lay on my bed my wedding sheets. Remember.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: And call thy husband hither.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Here’s a change indeed.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: ’Tis meet I should be used so, very meet.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: How have I been behaved that he might stick

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: The small’st opinion on my least misuse?

speaker: IAGO
text: What is your pleasure, madam? How is ’t with you?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I cannot tell. Those that do teach young babes

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Do it with gentle means and easy tasks.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: He might have chid me so, for, in good faith,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I am a child to chiding.

speaker: IAGO
text: What is the matter, lady?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Alas, Iago, my lord hath so bewhored her,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Thrown such despite and heavy terms upon her

speaker: EMILIA
text: As true hearts cannot bear.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Am I that name, Iago?

speaker: IAGO
text: What name, fair lady?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Such as she said my lord did say I was.

speaker: EMILIA
text: He called her “whore.” A beggar in his drink

speaker: EMILIA
text: Could not have laid such terms upon his callet.

speaker: IAGO
text: Why did he so?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I do not know. I am sure I am none such.

speaker: IAGO
text: Do not weep, do not weep! Alas the day!

speaker: EMILIA
text: Hath she forsook so many noble matches,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Her father and her country and her friends,

speaker: EMILIA
text: To be called “whore”? Would it not make one weep?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: It is my wretched fortune.

speaker: IAGO
text: Beshrew him for ’t! How comes this trick upon him?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Nay, heaven doth know.

speaker: EMILIA
text: I will be hanged if some eternal villain,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Some busy and insinuating rogue,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Some cogging, cozening slave, to get some office,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Have not devised this slander. I will be hanged else.

speaker: IAGO
text: Fie, there is no such man. It is impossible.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: If any such there be, heaven pardon him.

speaker: EMILIA
text: A halter pardon him, and hell gnaw his bones!

speaker: EMILIA
text: Why should he call her “whore”? Who keeps her company?

speaker: EMILIA
text: What place? What time? What form? What likelihood?

speaker: EMILIA
text: The Moor’s abused by some most villainous knave,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Some base notorious knave, some scurvy fellow.

speaker: EMILIA
text: O heaven, that such companions thou ’dst unfold,

speaker: EMILIA
text: And put in every honest hand a whip

speaker: EMILIA
text: To lash the rascals naked through the world,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Even from the east to th’ west!

speaker: IAGO
text: Speak within door.

speaker: EMILIA
text: O, fie upon them! Some such squire he was

speaker: EMILIA
text: That turned your wit the seamy side without

speaker: EMILIA
text: And made you to suspect me with the Moor.

speaker: IAGO
text: You are a fool. Go to!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Alas, Iago,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: What shall I do to win my lord again?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Good friend, go to him. For by this light of heaven,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text:
I know not how I lost him.
                 
                She
                 
                kneels
                .
               Here I kneel.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: If e’er my will did trespass ’gainst his love,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Either in discourse of thought or actual deed,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Or that mine eyes, mine ears, or any sense

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Delighted them in any other form,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Or that I do not yet, and ever did,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: And ever will—though he do shake me off

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: To beggarly divorcement—love him dearly,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text:
Comfort forswear me!
                 
                She
                 
                stands
                .
               Unkindness may do much,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: And his unkindness may defeat my life,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: But never taint my love. I cannot say “whore”—

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: It does abhor me now I speak the word.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: To do the act that might the addition earn,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Not the world’s mass of vanity could make me.

speaker: IAGO
text: I pray you be content. ’Tis but his humor.

speaker: IAGO
text: The business of the state does him offense,

speaker: IAGO
text: And he does chide with you.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: If ’twere no other—

speaker: IAGO
text: It is but so, I warrant.

speaker: IAGO
text: Hark how these instruments summon to supper.

speaker: IAGO
text: The messengers of Venice stays the meat.

speaker: IAGO
text: Go in and weep not. All things shall be well.

speaker: IAGO
text: How now, Roderigo?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I do not find

speaker: RODERIGO
text: That thou deal’st justly with me.

speaker: IAGO
text: What in the contrary?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Every day thou daff’st me with some device,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Iago, and rather, as it seems to me now,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: keep’st from me all conveniency than suppliest me

speaker: RODERIGO
text: with the least advantage of hope. I will indeed no

speaker: RODERIGO
text: longer endure it. Nor am I yet persuaded to put up

speaker: RODERIGO
text: in peace what already I have foolishly suffered.

speaker: IAGO
text: Will you hear me, Roderigo?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Faith, I have heard too much, and your

speaker: RODERIGO
text: words and performances are no kin together.

speaker: IAGO
text: You charge me most unjustly.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: With naught but truth. I have wasted myself

speaker: RODERIGO
text: out of my means. The jewels you have had

speaker: RODERIGO
text: from me to deliver to Desdemona would half have

speaker: RODERIGO
text: corrupted a votaress. You have told me she hath

speaker: RODERIGO
text: received them, and returned me expectations and

speaker: RODERIGO
text: comforts of sudden respect and acquaintance, but I

speaker: RODERIGO
text: find none.

speaker: IAGO
text: Well, go to! Very well.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: “Very well.” “Go to!” I cannot go to, man,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: nor ’tis not very well! By this hand, I say ’tis very

speaker: RODERIGO
text: scurvy, and begin to find myself fopped in it.

speaker: IAGO
text: Very well.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I tell you ’tis not very well! I will make

speaker: RODERIGO
text: myself known to Desdemona. If she will return me

speaker: RODERIGO
text: my jewels, I will give over my suit and repent my

speaker: RODERIGO
text: unlawful solicitation. If not, assure yourself I will

speaker: RODERIGO
text: seek satisfaction of you.

speaker: IAGO
text: You have said now.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Ay, and said nothing but what I protest

speaker: RODERIGO
text: intendment of doing.

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, now I see there’s mettle in thee, and even

speaker: IAGO
text: from this instant do build on thee a better opinion

speaker: IAGO
text: than ever before. Give me thy hand, Roderigo.

speaker: IAGO
text: Thou hast taken against me a most just exception,

speaker: IAGO
text: but yet I protest I have dealt most directly in thy

speaker: IAGO
text: affair.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: It hath not appeared.

speaker: IAGO
text: I grant indeed it hath not appeared, and your

speaker: IAGO
text: suspicion is not without wit and judgment. But,

speaker: IAGO
text: Roderigo, if thou hast that in thee indeed which I

speaker: IAGO
text: have greater reason to believe now than ever—I

speaker: IAGO
text: mean purpose, courage, and valor—this night show

speaker: IAGO
text: it. If thou the next night following enjoy not Desdemona,

speaker: IAGO
text: take me from this world with treachery and

speaker: IAGO
text: devise engines for my life.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Well, what is it? Is it within reason and

speaker: RODERIGO
text: compass?

speaker: IAGO
text: Sir, there is especial commission come from

speaker: IAGO
text: Venice to depute Cassio in Othello’s place.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Is that true? Why, then, Othello and Desdemona

speaker: RODERIGO
text: return again to Venice.

speaker: IAGO
text: O, no. He goes into Mauritania and takes away

speaker: IAGO
text: with him the fair Desdemona, unless his abode be

speaker: IAGO
text: lingered here by some accident—wherein none

speaker: IAGO
text: can be so determinate as the removing of Cassio.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: How do you mean, removing him?

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, by making him uncapable of Othello’s

speaker: IAGO
text: place: knocking out his brains.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: And that you would have me to do?

speaker: IAGO
text: Ay, if you dare do yourself a profit and a right. He

speaker: IAGO
text: sups tonight with a harlotry, and thither will I go to

speaker: IAGO
text: him. He knows not yet of his honorable fortune. If

speaker: IAGO
text: you will watch his going thence (which I will

speaker: IAGO
text: fashion to fall out between twelve and one), you may

speaker: IAGO
text: take him at your pleasure. I will be near to second

speaker: IAGO
text: your attempt, and he shall fall between us. Come,

speaker: IAGO
text: stand not amazed at it, but go along with me. I will

speaker: IAGO
text: show you such a necessity in his death that you shall

speaker: IAGO
text: think yourself bound to put it on him. It is now high

speaker: IAGO
text: supper time, and the night grows to waste. About it!

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I will hear further reason for this.

speaker: IAGO
text: And you shall be satisfied.
